name: Dyon
description: A rusty dynamically typed scripting language designed for game engines and interactive applications

language-family.comment: Inspired by JavaScript, Go, and Rust; unique syntax with mathematical expressions and go-like coroutines

first-release.comment: Sven Nilsen started the project in early 2016

typing.comment: Dynamically typed with optional ad-hoc type annotations; runtime type checking on mutation

paradigm.comment: Supports imperative style with functional elements; mathematical function expressions

first-class-functions.comment: Functions can be passed as values; supports closures

closures.comment: Closures with grab expressions for capturing variables

pattern-matching.comment: No dedicated pattern matching syntax; uses if/else and type checking

error-handling.comment: Result and Option types with ? operator for error propagation; unwrap() prints trace of propagated errors

async-await.comment: No native async/await syntax; uses Go-like coroutines with 'go' keyword instead

iterators-generators.comment: Various for loop styles including mathematical loops (sum, product, min, max), infer range

module-system.comment: Dynamic modules with runtime loading; optional namespaces with shared aliases

standard-library.comment: Includes file I/O, network (HTTP), threading, math, vector/matrix operations, type checking

execution-strategy.comment: Direct interpretation; lifetime checking eliminates need for garbage collector

compilation-model.comment: Scripts parsed and executed at runtime via load() function

hot-reloading.comment: Dynamic module loading enables script reloading; piston-dyon_interactive crate for live coding

debugging-support.comment: debug_lookup feature flag available but no full debugger interface (breakpoints, step-through)

source-maps.comment: Error traces include source locations; unwrap() prints propagation trace

startup-time.comment: Pure Rust with no JIT warmup; designed for interactive game scripting

execution-speed.comment: Interpreted without bytecode VM; designed for game scripting where simplicity matters more than raw speed

memory-overhead.comment: No garbage collector; uses lifetime checking for memory management

garbage-collection.comment: Lifetime checker eliminates need for garbage collection; RAII-like semantics

api-safety.comment: Safe public API; dyon_fn! macro handles unsafe internally

idiomatic-api.comment: Implementation straightforward but API took time to figure out; workable but not as intuitive as alternatives

serde-integration.comment: Uses custom data format (Dyon data); no native serde support

async-compatible.comment: Primary model is sync with Go-like coroutines; optional tokio feature available

function-registration.comment: dyon_fn! macro for function registration; dyon_obj! for custom objects

type-mapping.comment: Variable enum for type handling; manual conversion between Rust and Dyon types

no-std-compatible.comment: Requires std for file I/O, networking, threading features

wasm-compatible.comment: Default features (rand, http/reqwest) marked 'not wasm'; would require significant feature disabling

blank-slate-mode.comment: Feature flags (stdio, file, http, threading) allow removing capabilities at compile time

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory; lifetime checking enforces boundaries

cpu-limits.comment: No documented instruction count or execution time limits

memory-limits.comment: No garbage collector means no configurable memory limits; relies on lifetime checking

syscall-restrictions.comment: Feature flags control file I/O (file), stdio, networking (http), and threading access

capability-security.comment: Compile-time feature flags control what capabilities scripts can access

default-deny.comment: Default features include rand and http; must explicitly disable for sandboxing

share-data.comment: Data passed via Variable enum; copy-on-write semantics for efficiency

custom-types.comment: dyon_obj! macro for exposing Rust objects to scripts

operator-overloading.comment: Built-in operators for vectors/matrices but no custom operator definition

method-syntax.comment: Dot notation for method calls on objects

callbacks-events.comment: Functions are first-class; closures can be used as callbacks

package-manager.comment: No package ecosystem; modules loaded via load() function

editor-support.comment: No documented editor extensions, LSP, or syntax highlighting packages

example-projects.comment: Part of Piston game engine ecosystem; designed for game scripting

maintenance-status.comment: Recent release December 2025; 90 versions published; 14 contributors

documentation-quality.comment: 100% of crate documented; tutorial available at piston.rs; DYON-API documentation format

maturity.comment: Active since 2016; 90 versions; used in Piston game engine projects

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed specifically for game engines and interactive applications; 4D vectors/matrices for graphics
